title: F-OAUTH-DETECTOR-MAC-PATH: oauth_refresh_detector Mac branch — Keychain mdat polling (cycle 2 fan-out 2/5)
predecessor_witnesses: nexus/.raw-audit ts=2026-04-28T04:50:19Z 'detector-false-positive-fixed-queue-bug-discovered' (previous fix swapped .claude.json → .credentials.json; this fix swaps file-mtime → Keychain mdat for darwin)
scope: Fix oauth_refresh_detector body's Mac path so REFRESH_HEALTHY can fire on Mac. Current logic watches ~/.claude-claude9/.credentials.json mtime, but Mac CLI stores OAuth in macOS Keychain item 'Claude Code-credentials' — file mtime never changes, so signal never fires. Add OS branching: darwin uses Keychain mdat, linux/other keep file mtime.

current_state_evidence.credentials_file_mtime_on_mac: Apr 21 05:11 (1776715872) — frozen ~7 days, last modified before authoring of this fix
current_state_evidence.keychain_mdat_on_mac: 20260427024539Z (Apr 27 02:45 UTC) — actively maintained by Mac CLI, advanced 6 days past file mtime
current_state_evidence.log_evidence_pre_fix: /Users/ghost/.hx/log/oauth_refresh_events.log shows 1 spurious MAC_REFRESH at 2026-04-28T04:47:51Z (file touched once externally) + zero genuine REFRESH_HEALTHY events despite Mac CLI being used continuously
current_state_evidence.linux_side_status: WORKING — hetzner /root/.claude-claude1/.claude.json mtime correctly tracked via ssh probe (300s cadence)

investigation_summary.root_cause: raw 91 C3 — file-based detection assumes credential persistence through filesystem; Mac CLI uses Keychain CRUD via security framework, leaving credentials.json untouched after initial profile bootstrap.
investigation_summary.fix_layer: tool/oauth_refresh_detector.body — POSIX shell daemon body, locked with chflags uchg per raw 9 hexa-only mandate

diff_summary: Added mac_keychain_mdat() function: `security find-generic-password -s 'Claude Code-credentials'` (NO -w flag → secret never read), pipe through `grep '"mdat"'` and `sed -n 's/.*"\([0-9]\{14\}\)Z.*/\1/p'`. Returns 14-digit YYYYMMDDHHMMSS string or empty.
Bootstrap branch by $OS: Darwin tries Keychain first; if mdat empty, falls back to file mode (F-3 falsifier).
Main loop polls mdat string equality (not mtime numeric); on mid-run mdat disappearance, switches MAC_MODE to file and continues without flapping.
Linux/other branch unchanged — uses existing stat -f %m file mtime path.
REFRESH_HEALTHY logic now driven by LAST_REFRESH_TS (set in either Mac branch) rather than LAST_MAC_MTIME directly — keeps healthy-window semantics when Mac source is mdat string.
patch_applied.secret_safety: security CLI invoked WITHOUT `-w`. stdout contains attribute metadata only (cdat, mdat, svce, acct). Verified by selftest grep for access_token/refresh_token/sk-ant/ya29 → 0 hits.

isolated_helper_test.missing_key_returns_empty: PASS — non-existent service name returns empty string cleanly

live_smoke_test.method: ran full body for 3s in subshell with redirected log path; verified bootstrap line
live_smoke_test.verdict: PASS — new format emitted, old 'START mac_mtime=...' format gone

launchd_daemon_restart.verdict: PASS — daemon picked up patched body, branched into keychain mode, hetzner probe still functional

raw_71_falsifiers.name: Keychain mdat unchanged for >24h despite Mac CLI usage
raw_71_falsifiers.predicate: If `tail /Users/ghost/.hx/log/oauth_refresh_events.log | grep MAC_REFRESH` shows zero MAC_REFRESH lines in any 24h window AND `claude -p ok` succeeds in that window AND `security find-generic-password -s 'Claude Code-credentials' | grep mdat` shows mdat advanced from logged value, then this fix is broken (parsing or polling cadence regression).
raw_71_falsifiers.remedy_if_fails: verify sed regex against current `security` output format (Apple may change escape sequence); add tee-to-stderr debug line to mac_keychain_mdat()

raw_71_falsifiers.name: false-positive: linux host running Mac branch
raw_71_falsifiers.predicate: If detector deployed to hetzner / ubu* (linux uname=Linux) and emits `START_MAC_KEYCHAIN` line instead of `START_LINUX`, OS branching is broken.
raw_71_falsifiers.check_command: ssh hetzner 'tail -20 ~/.hx/log/oauth_refresh_events.log 2>/dev/null | grep START_MAC_KEYCHAIN' (expect zero hits)
raw_71_falsifiers.remedy_if_fails: verify case statement; security CLI does not exist on linux so it would fail-empty anyway, but explicit branching is preferred

raw_71_falsifiers.name: mdat field absent → fallback path
raw_71_falsifiers.predicate: If Apple removes/renames mdat attribute in future macOS versions OR Keychain item is deleted/renamed, mac_keychain_mdat returns empty. Fix MUST fall through to file mode and emit START_MAC_FALLBACK_FILE log line.
raw_71_falsifiers.check_command: security delete-generic-password -s 'TEST-NONEXISTENT' 2>/dev/null; mac_keychain_mdat against fake service → empty
raw_71_falsifiers.selftest_status: PASS — non-existent service returns empty; bootstrap branches to MAC_MODE=file with START_MAC_FALLBACK_FILE
raw_71_falsifiers.remedy_if_fails: add second fallback to /Users/ghost/Library/Application\ Support/Claude/* config tree mtime

raw_91_c3_honest_limits: Keychain `mdat` is updated whenever ANY attribute write happens to the Keychain item — could in theory be touched by something other than OAuth refresh (e.g., Keychain Access GUI 'Show password' user prompt, ACL edits). Probability low but not zero. False-positive MAC_REFRESH is benign because REFRESH_HEALTHY also requires hetzner refresh within 30min.

We did NOT use `security find-generic-password -w` — that flag prints the secret to stdout. Reading mdat metadata only is safer but still requires the daemon to have keychain access (sandbox/launchd userid context must match). Verified working under launchd gui/$(id -u) bootstrap on this Mac (PID 53340 emitted bootstrap line).

Alternative considered: `log stream --predicate 'subsystem == "com.apple.security"'` for syslog event detection. Rejected — requires sudo, produces high-volume noise (every Keychain access by any app), no clean filter for the specific 'Claude Code-credentials' item, and would be killed by ThrottleInterval=30s launchd policy.

MAC_MODE switch is one-way: keychain → file on mid-run mdat-missing. Does NOT switch back to keychain if Keychain item is recreated. Mitigation: KeepAlive=true causes daemon restart on crash; daemon also restarts cleanly on launchctl kickstart. Acceptable.
Polling cadence stays at 60s same as before. Keychain CLI invocation is ~5–20ms wall-time on M-series Mac (selftest measured), well within budget.

consumers_affected: pending_kick_replay_daemon (downstream of REFRESH_HEALTHY events) — should now actually fire on Mac side
nexus hexa_remote oauth blacklist clearer — same

outstanding_followups: monitor next 24h for first Mac MAC_REFRESH event (Mac CLI typically refreshes tokens every few hours)
if F-OAUTH-DETECTOR-MAC-PATH-1 fires, dump raw `security find-generic-password` output to stderr for one cycle to inspect format drift
